event: 13-year-old girl
DiGeorge syndrome
prior neonatal truncus arteriosus repair
severe left pulmonary artery hypoplasia
methicillin-sensitive staphylococcal bacteremia
infective endocarditis
septic pulmonary emboli
bilateral pyelonephritis
septic shock
worsening respiratory failure
pulmonary hemorrhage
placed on venovenous extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (V-V ECMO)
acute hemorrhage noted around and in the right chest tube
acute hemorrhage noted in the endotracheal tube
computed tomography angiography showed patent conduit between right ventricle and pulmonary artery
computed tomography angiography showed acute right hemothorax
computed tomography angiography showed 19-mm bilobed presumed mycotic aneurysm
mycotic aneurysm involving medial basal segment of right lower lobe pulmonary artery
extravasation noted from intercostal artery at site of right chest tube
general anesthesia
left common femoral vein access obtained antegrade using micropuncture set
9-French, 70 cm Flexor sheath placed into inferior vena cava
7-French APC catheter used to select main pulmonary artery
digital subtraction arteriography revealed patent pulmonary artery conduit
digital subtraction arteriography confirmed 19-mm bilobed mycotic aneurysm
guiding sheath advanced into right pulmonary artery
5-French, 100 cm vertebral tip catheter used
angled tip Glidewire used to cannulate branch vessel feeding mycotic aneurysm
decision to occlude mycotic aneurysm using balloon occlusion technique with EVOH (Onyx)
150-cm Scepter C Occlusion Balloon Catheter advanced coaxially
occlusion balloon placed at neck of mycotic aneurysm
occlusion balloon inflated to 5 mm
catheter primed with dimethyl sulfoxide
EVOH (Onyx 34) injected into mycotic aneurysm
mycotic aneurysm filled completely
balloon deflated
repeat pulmonary arteriography showed occlusion of mycotic aneurysm
preserved flow to normal lung parenchyma
right femoral arterial access obtained
thoracic aortogram revealed extravasation from right intercostal artery
intercostal artery treated with coils and Gelfoam
total procedure time 132 minutes
patient returned to pediatric intensive care unit
hemorrhage into right chest tube resolved
hemorrhage in endotracheal tube resolved
care withdrawn due to neurologic deterioration
family declined autopsy
